Title: Biosynthesis and regulation of salicylic acid and N-hydroxypypecolic acid in plant immunity

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for pathogen-induced SA accumulation and also for NHP formation, likely by exporting isochorismate and Pip from plastids to cytosol.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by pathogen infection; coordinately regulated with ICS1 and PBS3
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; subcellular localization; UV and pathogen-induced metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EDS5 is a chloroplast-envelope MATE transporter essential for SA accumulation. 2. It most likely exports isochorismate for cytosolic SA formation. 3. It is also required for NHP biosynthesis, likely by exporting Pip from plastids to the cytosol.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Immune defense and systemic acquired resistance
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts in both SA and NHP pathways, making it a shared transport node.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation causes strong disease susceptibility due to failure to accumulate defense metabolites.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Shared transport hub for two immunity metabolites; useful target for coordinated pathway enhancement, but broad immune effects require caution.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts SA to SAG; loss reduces SAG and elevates SA, enhancing disease resistance.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT74F1 participates in SA glucosylation to form SAG. 2. Loss of function reduces SAG accumulation. 3. Reduced glucosylation increases free SA and disease resistance.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts in SA homeostasis rather than core de novo biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Elevated SA in mutants may incur defense-growth tradeoffs.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A candidate for elevating active SA by reducing glycosylation, though constitutive defense costs should be considered.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to SA glucosylation; loss reduces SAG and increases SA, enhancing disease resistance.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT76B1 contributes to conversion of SA to SAG. 2. Loss reduces SAG accumulation and elevates free SA. 3. This leads to enhanced resistance in Arabidopsis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reported in the review as one of the UGTs involved in SA-to-SAG conversion.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May shift SA homeostasis and growth-defense balance.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential editing target for increasing active SA pools.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Hydroxylates SA to 2,3-DHBA and contributes to SA catabolism, especially during senescence.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Very low in young plants; primarily functions during senescence
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. S3H converts SA to 2,3-DHBA. 2. It functions mainly during senescence. 3. Together with S5H it contributes to SA homeostasis and immunity modulation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: s5h s3h double mutants accumulate more SA than s5h alone.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing SA catabolism may intensify defense and alter senescence.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible target to elevate SA, particularly in age-related immunity contexts.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Hydroxylates SA to 2,5-DHBA; loss elevates SA and enhances pathogen resistance.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single and double mutant analysis; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. S5H/DMR6 converts SA to 2,5-DHBA. 2. The s5h/dmr6 mutant accumulates elevated SA. 3. Increased SA correlates with enhanced pathogen resistance, further enhanced in s5h s3h double mutants.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Partially redundant with S3H but functionally distinct.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Elevated SA due to loss may affect growth-defense balance.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important target for disease resistance engineering through SA catabolism attenuation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts chorismate to isochorismate in plastids.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: major committed-step enzyme for pathogen-induced SA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes pathogen-induced salicylic acid accumulation; loss largely blocks pathogen-induced SA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by pathogen infection; constitutively elevated in autoimmune mutants snc1 and snc2-1D
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis (sid2/ics1, ics1 ics2); pathogen-induced metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ICS1 was identified as SID2 and encodes isochorismate synthase. 2. It plays the major role in pathogen-induced SA biosynthesis in Arabidopsis. 3. It functions in plastids to generate isochorismate for downstream SA production.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling; essential for basal defense and systemic acquired resistance
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Local and distal leaves during immune responses
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Arabidopsis has two ICS genes; ICS1 has the dominant role over ICS2 in pathogen-induced SA production.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts chorismate to isochorismate in plastids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Minor contribution to pathogen-induced salicylic acid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ICS2 encodes a plastidial isochorismate synthase. 2. It contributes less than ICS1 to pathogen-induced SA accumulation. 3. SA levels are further reduced in ics1 ics2 compared with ics1 alone.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling; basal defense and systemic acquired resistance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts redundantly but more weakly than ICS1.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: GH3 acyl adenylase family protein PBS3
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes conjugation of glutamate to isochorismate to form isochorismate-9-glutamate, which subsequently decomposes to salicylic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step in plant-specific ICS pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes pathogen-induced salicylic acid accumulation; mutations reduce SA levels.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by bacterial pathogen; coordinately regulated with ICS1 and EDS5
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PBS3 is required for full pathogen-induced SA accumulation. 2. It conjugates glutamate to isochorismate in the cytosol. 3. The product isochorismate-9-glutamate spontaneously decays to produce SA.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling and systemic acquired resistance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Group III GH3 protein, mostly found in Brassicaceae.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Stimulates the decay of isochorismate-9-glutamate to salicylic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Boosts SA production by enhancing breakdown of isochorismate-9-glutamate.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EPS1 was identified from the eps1 SA-deficient mutant. 2. It enhances conversion of isochorismate-9-glutamate toward SA. 3. This boosting mechanism is newly evolved in Brassicaceae.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana tabacum; Glycine max; Oryza sativa
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana tabacum; Glycine max; Oryza sativa
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts phenylalanine to trans-cinnamic acid, the first step of the PAL route contributing to SA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to basal and pathogen-induced SA biosynthesis; loss of all four Arabidopsis PAL genes strongly reduces SA.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Isotope labeling; inhibitor treatment; mutant analysis of Arabidopsis PAL family
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PALs initiate the PAL branch of SA biosynthesis by converting Phe to trans-cinnamic acid. 2. In tobacco, pathogen-induced SA is suggested to derive primarily from the PAL pathway. 3. In Arabidopsis, loss of all four PAL genes reduces both basal and pathogen-induced SA.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pathogen infection; PAL inhibitor AIP treatment
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PAL pathway contribution is species dependent and works alongside ICS pathway in some plants.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: PAL genes also feed general phenylpropanoid metabolism, so perturbation may broadly affect development and stress responses.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Mediates conversion of trans-cinnamic acid to benzoic acid through beta-oxidation-related reactions.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes BA and SA formation in the PAL pathway; aim1 mutants accumulate more cinnamic acid and less BA/SA.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant metabolite profiling in rice aim1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AIM1 is involved in beta-oxidation and supports conversion of CA to BA. 2. Rice aim1 mutants accumulate elevated CA but reduced BA and SA. 3. This supports AIM1 action in the second step of the PAL pathway toward SA.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In rice, AIM1-mediated SA biosynthesis contributes to maintaining proper ROS levels for root meristem activity.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: AIM1 has developmental roles; mutation causes abnormal inflorescence development in Arabidopsis.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Uses lysine as substrate and catalyzes formation of ε-amino-α-keto caproic acid, which spontaneously cyclizes to Δ1-piperidine-2-carboxylic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: essential upstream enzyme for Pip and NHP production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for Pip and NHP biosynthesis and for systemic acquired resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by bacterial pathogen; reduced in sard1 cbp60g and wrky33 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: SAR-deficient mutant analysis; biochemical pathway elucidation; heterologous reconstitution with SARD4 in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ALD1 encodes an aminotransferase essential for onset of SAR. 2. It catalyzes the first step from lysine toward pipecolic acid. 3. Loss of ALD1 compromises Pip/NHP accumulation and systemic immunity.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling and systemic acquired resistance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ald1 mutants retain only slight local SA reduction but completely lose distal SA accumulation during SAR establishment.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces Δ1-piperidine-2-carboxylic acid to pipecolic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for full Pip accumulation, especially in distal leaves, and contributes to NHP production and SAR.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by pathogen infection; reduced in sard1 cbp60g mutant
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: sard4 mutant analysis; biochemical reconstitution in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SARD4 catalyzes reduction of P2C to Pip. 2. It is required for Pip accumulation in distal leaves and contributes in local tissue. 3. ALD1 and SARD4 are sufficient to reconstitute the Pip pathway in E. coli.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review notes another reductase may also work with ALD1 in Pip production beyond SARD4.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes N-hydroxylation of pipecolic acid to yield N-hydroxypipecolic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for NHP formation; fmo1 mutants accumulate Pip but fail to produce NHP.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by bacterial pathogen; reduced in sard1 cbp60g and wrky33 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FMO1 is required for pathogen-induced NHP accumulation. 2. It converts Pip to NHP by N-hydroxylation. 3. Loss of FMO1 causes Pip accumulation without detectable NHP.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Close homologs exist in most plants, suggesting conservation of NHP biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ICS1; EDS5; PBS3; ALD1; SARD4; FMO1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates expression of SA and NHP biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master immune transcription factor coordinating SA and NHP biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced upon pathogen infection and by salicylic acid; elevated in autoimmune contexts
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Pathogen infection; SA; Pip/NHP; TGA1/TGA4 positive input; WRKY70 and CAMTA factors negative input
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes SA, Pip and NHP accumulation and systemic acquired resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutant; overexpression; ChIP/ChIP-seq; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SARD1 directly targets genes in both SA and NHP biosynthetic pathways. 2. Together with CBP60g, it is required for most pathogen-induced SA accumulation and strongly promotes Pip/NHP biosynthesis. 3. It serves as a central node in positive feedback amplification by SA and Pip/NHP.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Immune defense and systemic acquired resistance
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive feedback via induction by SA and Pip/NHP
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Overexpression of SARD1 is sufficient to activate ICS1, ALD1 and SARD4.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Negative regulator of some resistance protein-mediated autoimmunity contexts was also noted.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A strong candidate master regulator for boosting broad immune metabolite accumulation, but constitutive activation may risk autoimmunity or growth costs.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CBP60 family with N-terminal CaM-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ICS1; EDS5; PBS3; ALD1; SARD4; FMO1
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation modulated post-transcriptionally by Ca2+/CaM
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Core positive regulator coordinating pathogen-induced SA and NHP biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced upon pathogen infection; induced by Pip/NHP
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Pathogen infection; TGA1/TGA4; GTL1; CAMTA negative control; Ca2+/CaM
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes pathogen-induced SA, Pip and NHP accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; ChIP/ChIP-seq; CaM-binding domain functional tests
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CBP60g works redundantly with SARD1 to activate SA and NHP biosynthetic genes. 2. Its CaM-binding domain is required for promoting ICS1 expression and SA accumulation. 3. It integrates calcium signaling into immune metabolite biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive feedback via induction by Pip/NHP
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unlike SARD1, overexpression of CBP60g alone is not sufficient to induce SA/NHP biosynthetic genes, indicating post-transcriptional control.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive activation could potentially disturb calcium-regulated defenses.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful immune-metabolism regulator, but engineering should consider calcium/CaM dependence rather than simple overexpression.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates ALD1 directly and promotes FMO1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation downstream of MPK3/MPK6 phosphorylation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator linking MAPK signaling to Pip/NHP accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes Pip and NHP accumulation during defense responses.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; ChIP-PCR; MAPK activation assays; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WRKY33 directly binds the ALD1 promoter. 2. It is required for full induction of ALD1/FMO1 and accumulation of Pip/NHP during pathogen responses. 3. It functions in an MPK3/6-WRKY33-ALD1-Pip regulatory loop contributing to SAR.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Participates in regulatory loop with MPK3/6 and Pip
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pst DC3000 avrRpt2; Psm ES4326 infection
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Loss of WRKY33 elevates SA during Botrytis cinerea infection, indicating pathway crosstalk.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May differentially affect SA versus NHP branches depending on pathogen context.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to strengthen SAR via NHP, but cross-regulation with other defense programs should be monitored.
